nodes.name: Convert PDF to Page Images (HTTP)
nodes.notes: Replace YOUR_VERCEL_DOMAIN with your deployed app's domain (e.g. scgjwd-pricingv-2.vercel.app). Calls the api/pdf-to-images.js serverless function, which uses mupdf (WASM, no native deps) to rasterize every PDF page to a PNG and returns { totalPages, pages: [{page, totalPages, dataUrl}] }. This avoids needing Execute Command, which is disabled on n8n Cloud / managed instances.

nodes.name: Tag PDF Page Metadata
nodes.notes: Runs once per page image returned by the HTTP conversion endpoint. Attaches documentId/runId/fileName plus this page's number and the total page count so results can be split per page downstream.

nodes.name: Merge Pages
nodes.notes: Combines the PDF-page branch and the single-image branch back into one stream of page items, each item = one page to OCR.

nodes.name: Aggregate Pages Into Final Result
nodes.notes: Collects all per-page OCR outputs, sorts by page number, merges scalar fields (first page that has a value wins) and concatenates line_items + raw_text across pages. Keeps the full per-page breakdown in 'pages' for the frontend.

nodes.name: Save OCR Result to Supabase
nodes.notes: Stores the per-page breakdown in the existing 'metadata' jsonb column on ocr_results — no schema migration needed.

parameters.responseBody: ={ "success": true, "runId": "{{ $('Aggregate Pages Into Final Result').item.json.runId }}", "totalPages": {{ $('Aggregate Pages Into Final Result').item.json.totalPages }} }

parameters.content:
## Prerequisites for multi-page PDF support
1. **Deploy api/pdf-to-images.js to Vercel** (uses the `mupdf` WASM package — no native deps, works on n8n Cloud since it avoids Execute Command which is disabled there). Update the 'Convert PDF to Page Images (HTTP)' node's URL with your real Vercel domain.
2. No Supabase schema migration needed — per-page results are stored in the existing `ocr_results.metadata` jsonb column as `{ total_pages, pages: [{page, extracted_data, raw_text}] }`.
3. Each PDF page is rendered at 200 DPI and OCR'd independently with GPT-4o-mini Vision, then merged: scalar fields take the first page that has a value, line_items and raw_text are concatenated across all pages.
4. Vercel function caps at 30 pages per PDF — raise MAX_PAGES in api/pdf-to-images.js if you need more.
